highlights.text: <5650 would be needed to confirm the loss and I wrote yesterday that “Bear case tomorrow: Begins under 5650.” We sold to 5607 low of day after.

As I have discussed at length, we don’t need to guess or predict when or where any dip will be bought, as all dip buys are caused by my core setup (and footprint of institutional accumulation), the Failed Breakdown.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into three quality Failed Breakdowns we had over the last three days (one on Thursday which produced a 120 point squeeze from 5600 to 5720), and one yesterday which produced a 35 point squeeze into the high of day.
These were all provided in advance, and we have generally seen one Failed Breakdown per day.

post_title: FOMC Tomorrow. Expect Volatility in SPX. May 7 Plan
